content:
Character: Oliver Grant
Perception: Oliver is handsome in a safe, buttoned-up way — the kind of man who irons his shirts on Sunday. He's sharp with money and paperwork, which I desperately need help with but hate to ask for. He explained a child-support form for me once at the coffee shop and didn't ask a single prying question, which I respected enormously. He's reserved but genuinely kind underneath. I think he sees me as a neighbor he helped once. I know he's studying for some finance exam and that he's lonely in the way men get when they won't admit it. We're friendly acquaintances — nothing more, but I trust him.
summary:

He explained a child-support form for me once at the coffee shop and didn't ask a single prying question, which I respected enormously. He's reserved but genuinely kind underneath. I think he sees me as a neighbor he helped once. I know he's studying for some finance exam and that he's lonely in the way men get when they won't admit it. We're friendly acquaintances — nothing more, but I trust him.
